product: Endpoint Telemetry for Custom Model Serving
tagline: Now generally available for enhanced observability.
what_it_does: This feature allows users to configure endpoint telemetry for custom model serving endpoints, enabling the persistence of OpenTelemetry logs, traces, and metrics to Unity Catalog tables. It supports root cause analysis, monitoring of endpoint health, and compliance with SQL queries. Users can instrument their model code to capture telemetry data seamlessly.
why_it_matters: By providing detailed telemetry data, organizations can enhance their model performance monitoring, improve troubleshooting capabilities, and ensure compliance with data governance standards. This leads to better decision-making, reduced downtime, and increased trust in model outputs.
plain_summary: This feature allows users to configure endpoint telemetry for custom model serving endpoints, enabling the persistence of OpenTelemetry logs, traces, and metrics to Unity Catalog tables. It supports root cause analysis, monitoring of endpoint health, and…

capabilities.title: Telemetry Data Persistence
capabilities.desc: Persist OpenTelemetry logs, traces, and metrics to Unity Catalog tables.

capabilities.title: Root Cause Analysis
capabilities.desc: Utilize telemetry data for effective root cause analysis of model performance issues.

capabilities.title: Health Monitoring
capabilities.desc: Monitor the health of model serving endpoints through detailed telemetry insights.

capabilities.title: Compliance Support
capabilities.desc: Meet compliance requirements with standard SQL queries on telemetry data.

prerequisites: Workspace must be enabled for Unity Catalog.
Permissions: USE CATALOG, USE SCHEMA, CREATE TABLE, and MODIFY on the destination Unity Catalog.
An existing custom model serving endpoint or permissions to create one.
Workspace must be in a supported region.
use_cases: Identifying and troubleshooting errors in real-time.
Ensuring compliance with data governance policies.
Analyzing usage patterns for model endpoints.
limitations: Schema evolution on the target table is not supported.
Only managed Delta tables are supported.
The table location must be in the same region as your workspace.
Only single availability zone durability is supported.

steps.title: Instrument Model Code
steps.desc: Add logging and optional OpenTelemetry instrumentation to your model code.

steps.title: Prepare Unity Catalog
steps.desc: Ensure a catalog and schema are ready to receive telemetry data.

steps.title: Enable Telemetry
steps.desc: Configure telemetry settings when creating or updating an endpoint.

steps.title: Verify Telemetry Data
steps.desc: Check the Unity Catalog tables to ensure data is flowing correctly.

steps.title: Query Telemetry Data
steps.desc: Run SQL queries to analyze the telemetry data stored in Unity Catalog.

key_takeaway: Endpoint telemetry enhances observability for custom model serving, enabling better monitoring and compliance.
